elements.title: Gopal Mukund Huddar
elements.contents: ... that Indian anti-colonial activist and anti-fascist soldier Gopal Mukund Huddar (pictured) engaged in palm reading with his fellow inmates while imprisoned in a Francoist concentration camp?

elements.title: expatriate votes in Salvadoran elections
elements.contents: ... that expatriate votes in Salvadoran elections increased from fewer than 300 in 2009 to more than 300,000 in 2024?

elements.title: Laura Bowman
elements.contents: ... that Laura Bowman starred in the first sound horror film with an all-black cast?

elements.title: 2023 Jetline roller coaster accident
elements.contents: ... that in the 2023 Jetline roller coaster accident the lap bars bent so far out of position that three riders were ejected?

elements.title: Emmanuel Oyebadejo
elements.contents: ... that Emmanuel Oyebadejo discovered his affinity for American football while attending medical school in England?

elements.title: Reed Green Coliseum
elements.contents: ... that Reed Green Coliseum got its nickname, "The Yurt", from a dissatisfied ESPN writer?

elements.title: Eva Buzo
elements.contents: ... that Australian Eva Buzo swam the English Channel in 13 °C (55 °F) waters, breaking the women's world record for the earliest in-season channel crossing?

elements.title: robbed a Russian imperial treasury
elements.contents: ... that Georgian nationalist Leo Kereselidze and his associates robbed a Russian imperial treasury without anyone getting killed?

elements.title: Mint Fantôme
elements.contents: ... that Mint Fantôme once hosted a "Spit and Greet" at a convention?
